title: CI Matrices, Required Checks, Caching, and Merge Policy · Spiral Review
summary: Build a secure required-check workflow across supported environments. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a required CI quality-gate workflow after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate trusted branch workflows from untrusted pull-request code and secrets.
Order cheap discriminating checks before expensive matrices while preserving useful parallelism.
Test declared runtime, operating-system, database, and feature support intentionally.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Event Trust · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates ci required. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates ci required. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Event Trust without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate trusted branch workflows from untrusted pull-request code and secrets.

content.text: Separate trusted branch workflows from untrusted pull-request code and secrets. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates ci required. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Event Trust, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a required CI quality-gate workflow.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: All GitHub Actions events have the same trust model. This fails the Event Trust contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: TypeScript TSConfig Reference (TypeScript 5.9) — Controls strict typing, unused code checks, indexed access, and compiler gate behavior.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate trusted branch workflows from untrusted pull-request code and secrets.

stimulus.title: Required Status case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: gates ci required. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A workflow file automatically blocks unsafe merges.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Connect stable named checks to branch protection and define cancellation and rerun policy. Artifact under review: a required CI quality-gate workflow.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Required Status, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Required Status applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Connect stable named checks to branch protection and define cancellation and rerun policy.
